AcceptableUse.intro: This Acceptable Use Policy explains how CyberVPN may and may not be used across cyber-vpn.net, Telegram Bot, Telegram Mini App, customer web interfaces and VPN access provisioned through CyberVPN. cyber-vpn.org is reserved for VPN node and subscription delivery routes where CyberVPN presents them.

meta.description: Public Release 1.0 Acceptable Use Policy for CyberVPN.
meta.title: Acceptable Use Policy

items.personalSubscription: Use only the accounts, devices, subscription URLs, QR codes and configuration files that CyberVPN issued to you or explicitly authorized for your plan.
items.privateAccess: Use CyberVPN for lawful private network access, personal privacy, safer access on untrusted networks and availability of ordinary internet services where allowed by applicable law.
items.support: Contact support when a connection, payment, trial, renewal or provisioning issue prevents normal use.

changes.content: CyberVPN may update this policy when provider rules, node policies, abuse patterns, product flows or legal requirements change. Changes should stay aligned with the Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, Refund Policy and Cookie Policy.
changes.title: 7. Changes

items.controls: CyberVPN may warn, rate limit, throttle, block provisioning, suspend VPN access, revoke credentials, disable trials, terminate accounts or refuse renewal when abuse is detected or credibly reported.
items.evidence: CyberVPN may preserve account, payment, support, security and operational evidence needed to investigate abuse, comply with provider requirements, process disputes or satisfy legal obligations.
items.refunds: Refund handling is governed by the Refund Policy and support process. Abuse, fraud or policy violations may affect refund eligibility where allowed by applicable law.
items.review: Paid-but-no-access and abuse-related support cases are reviewed through the support escalation process. CyberVPN should avoid permanent action without enough evidence unless immediate action is needed to prevent harm.

items.automation: Automated high-volume traffic, mining, bot automation, scraping, port scanning, brute force workflows and load patterns that degrade the service may be limited or suspended.
items.configSafety: Do not publish raw subscription URLs, QR codes, private keys or configuration files in public channels. If credentials are exposed, CyberVPN may revoke and reissue them.
items.fairUse: Plans may be subject to fair-use limits, rate limits, device limits, traffic limits and anti-abuse controls to protect the service and other users.
items.nodeRules: Node-specific rules may be stricter than this public policy. If a provider, jurisdiction or rollout requires a node to block a traffic category, CyberVPN may enforce that rule without treating it as a breach of service.
items.torrent: CyberVPN does not promise that torrent or P2P traffic is available on every node. Torrent or P2P traffic may be blocked, throttled or restricted on nodes where provider policy, law, abuse risk or operational evidence requires it.

prohibited.intro: You may not use CyberVPN, CyberVPN accounts, VPN nodes, subscription URLs, QR codes or configuration files for any activity that harms CyberVPN, other users, providers or third parties. Prohibited activities include:

items.attacks: DDoS attacks, flooding, intrusion attempts, vulnerability exploitation, unauthorized port scanning, network reconnaissance or traffic intended to disrupt another service.
items.credentialStuffing: Credential stuffing, password spraying, brute force login attempts, automated account checking or any attack against authentication systems.
items.illegalContent: Accessing, storing, transmitting or helping distribute illegal content, child sexual abuse material, material exploiting minors, terrorist content or content that is unlawful in the relevant jurisdiction.
items.malware: Creating, hosting, distributing, controlling or helping malware, ransomware, botnets, command-and-control systems, exploit kits or malicious payloads.
items.phishing: Phishing, impersonation, social engineering, credential theft, account takeover, payment fraud, scams or attempts to collect sensitive data without authorization.
items.publicProxy: Running public proxies, open relays, exit nodes, reverse proxies, hosting services, crypto-mining infrastructure or server workloads through a personal CyberVPN account.
items.resale: Reselling, sharing, publicly posting or commercially redistributing accounts, subscription URLs, QR codes, configs or credentials unless CyberVPN explicitly approved that use in writing.
items.rightsAbuse: Copyright infringement, trademark abuse, counterfeit activity or other use that violates third-party rights where CyberVPN receives a valid complaint or provider requirement.
items.sanctions: Using the service in a way that violates applicable sanctions, export controls, payment-provider rules, hosting-provider rules or legal restrictions that apply to CyberVPN.
items.scraping: High-volume scraping, abusive crawling, spam SEO automation, ticket buying automation, fake engagement, content abuse or activity that violates another service's terms or rate limits.
items.spam: Sending spam, bulk unsolicited messages, abusive advertising, robocalls, mass account creation or similar messaging abuse.

prohibited.title: 3. Prohibited Activities

items.details: Reports should include timestamps, affected IPs or domains, relevant logs, message headers, account identifiers and a concise description of the suspected abuse where available.
items.supportEmail: For ordinary account, billing, trial or connection issues, contact support@cyber-vpn.net.

scope.content: This policy applies to all CyberVPN accounts, trials, paid subscriptions, support flows, configuration links, QR codes, subscription URLs and VPN credentials. If this policy conflicts with Terms of Service, Privacy Policy or provider-specific node rules, the stricter operational rule may be enforced until the documents are reconciled.
scope.title: 1. Scope

CookiePolicy.intro: This Cookie Policy explains cookies, local storage, session storage, telemetry and similar browser storage used or prepared in the CyberVPN web site, web cabinet, Telegram Mini App, backend API flows and admin/support surfaces.

meta.description: Public Release 1.0 Cookie Policy for CyberVPN.
meta.title: Cookie Policy

changes.content: CyberVPN may update this policy when storage mechanisms, SDKs, analytics providers, payment flows, login flows or legal requirements change. Changes should stay aligned with the Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Acceptable Use Policy and Refund Policy.
changes.title: 7. Changes

items.changes: If CyberVPN adds a new cookie, storage mechanism, SDK or tracking provider, the owner should approve the change and attach updated cookie inventory evidence before public use.
items.marketing: Marketing pixels, advertising retargeting or cross-site profiling require Cookie Policy, Privacy Policy, consent UI and evidence updates before public use.
items.necessary: Strictly necessary cookies and storage may be used to provide login, security, checkout return handling, fraud prevention, language routing, support and VPN provisioning flows requested by the user.
items.nonEssential: Non-essential analytics, marketing, profiling or advertising cookies must not be enabled by default unless the project has a consent mechanism, cookie inventory, legal approval and evidence.

items.browser: Users can control cookies, local storage and session storage in their browser or device settings. Blocking strictly necessary storage may prevent login, checkout, VPN provisioning, support/admin access or Telegram Mini App flows from working.
items.logout: Logging out should clear CyberVPN auth cookies. Some provider-side cookies, browser cache, locale preference or Telegram-controlled storage may need to be cleared through the browser, Telegram app or the third-party provider.
items.support: Cookie/privacy questions can be sent to support@cyber-vpn.net. Refund-related payment questions can be sent to refund@cyber-vpn.net.

items.browserInventory: Maintain deployed browser cookie inventory for public site, web cabinet, login/register, OAuth, Telegram Mini App, checkout, logout and admin flows.
items.consent: If non-essential analytics or marketing storage is enabled, maintain consent banner/settings evidence proving default-off behavior and withdrawal controls.
items.legal: Keep this Cookie Policy aligned with Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy and Refund Policy.
items.pii: Maintain Sentry/observability PII scrubbing evidence, analytics payload review and proof that cookies, Set-Cookie headers and secrets are filtered from logs.
items.setCookie: Maintain redacted Set-Cookie evidence proving Secure, HttpOnly where applicable, SameSite, domain, path and expiry/max-age for production HTTPS responses.

inventory.intro: The inventory is based on current product flows and deployed evidence expectations. Browser inventory should be refreshed when login, checkout, analytics, Telegram Mini App or admin flows change.

items.auth: Authentication cookies: the backend uses access_token, refresh_token and realm-scoped variants such as {realm}_access_token and {realm}_refresh_token for web API sessions. Production behavior should be HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Secure in production, path /api, default access lifetime 15 minutes and default refresh lifetime 7 days unless production configuration changes and evidence is updated.
items.locale: Language and routing state: next-intl may use a locale cookie such as NEXT_LOCALE where enabled to remember the selected language/locale. The exact production cookie name, domain, path and lifetime should match browser cookie inventory evidence.
items.observability: Operational observability may include Sentry error/performance events, Web Vitals, internal traffic analytics and Telegram Mini App runtime analytics. Sentry is configured with sendDefaultPii=false, text/media replay masking and beforeSend PII scrubbing where enabled.
items.telegram: Telegram Mini App flows may receive Telegram initData and Telegram WebView/runtime information. Cookies or storage controlled by Telegram itself are governed by Telegram, not by CyberVPN.
items.thirdParty: Payment providers, OAuth providers, Telegram, Sentry, GA4, PostHog or other analytics/observability services may set or access their own storage on their own domains only where that provider is enabled. PostHog, GA4, marketing pixels and advertising tracking are disabled unless separately approved, consented where required and evidenced.
items.transaction: Short-lived browser flow cookies may be used for OAuth, Magic link/OTP, 2FA and checkout return handling, including oauth_tx, pending_2fa and oauth_result. CSRF protection validates Origin/Referer for unsafe cookie-authenticated API requests.

inventory.title: 2. Cookie and Storage Inventory

items.auth: Auth cookie retention follows token configuration: access cookies default to 15 minutes and refresh cookies default to 7 days unless production configuration changes and evidence is updated.
items.locale: Locale and preference storage may remain until browser expiry, user clearing, logout or another configured lifetime confirmed by browser evidence.
items.telemetry: Sentry, Web Vitals, internal analytics and provider telemetry retention must follow the Privacy Policy, provider settings and observability evidence.
items.transactions: OAuth, Magic link/OTP, 2FA and checkout return cookies or browser state are intended to be short-lived and limited to completing the current login/payment flow.

scope.content: This policy covers cyber-vpn.net as the primary public domain and admin.cyber-vpn.net as the primary admin domain. cyber-vpn.org is reserved for VPN node and subscription delivery routes where CyberVPN presents them; it is not a general customer-site mirror. Cookies should stay canonical to cyber-vpn.net/admin.cyber-vpn.net unless a separate evidence pack approves another domain.

Features.description: The public release feature set focuses on login, trial or payment, Remnawave provisioning, config delivery, support recovery, and monitored operations.

killswitch.description: The product surface prioritizes recoverability: paid-but-no-access cases, provisioning failures, expired access, and support escalation have explicit handling.
killswitch.subtitle: Payment and provisioning safeguards
killswitch.title: Support Recovery Path

multihop.description: Regions are not public promises until staging or production node evidence exists. The catalogue hides unavailable nodes.
multihop.subtitle: Country-level public inventory
multihop.title: Evidence-Gated Regions

obfuscation.description: Reality-based profiles are designed for restricted networks, but public copy stays bounded: performance and access depend on the user's network and current node evidence.
obfuscation.subtitle: No universal unblock promise
obfuscation.title: DPI-Resistant Profiles

quantum.description: CyberVPN keeps the public VPN transport surface narrow: VLESS Reality RAW is the default profile and VLESS Reality XHTTP is the alternate path where configured and evidenced.
specs: Out of public default: Helix, Verta, Beep production
quantum.subtitle: VLESS Reality RAW + XHTTP
quantum.title: Protocol Baseline

items.desc: CyberVPN does not make an independent audited no-logs claim. Operational metadata may exist for security, billing, support, and provisioning.
items.title: Bounded Privacy Copy

items.desc: Device counts depend on the selected plan and must be enforced by the backend and support process.
items.title: Plan-Based Device Limits

items.desc: Automatic renewal appears only where the provider flow, user consent, cancel flow and evidence support it.
items.title: Renewal Controls

items.desc: Torrent/P2P access is not advertised as broadly available. Node policy and provider rules control what is enabled.
items.title: P2P Restricted by Default

Features.title: Public Release Capabilities

Footer.brandDescription: Public B2C VPN access with evidence-gated regions, bounded privacy claims, and monitored support recovery paths.

newsletter.description: Get updates on enabled regions, support notices, and release changes.

HelpCenter.categories_intro: Pick a verified module and jump straight to the relevant answers.
HelpCenter.category_billing_desc: Subscriptions, payments, refunds, and bandwidth allocation.
HelpCenter.category_getting_started_desc: Account setup and first connection guidance.
HelpCenter.category_security_desc: Transport profiles, evidence boundaries, privacy limits, and abuse controls.
HelpCenter.category_troubleshooting_desc: Diagnostics for connection and tunneling errors.
HelpCenter.contact_desc: If the help center does not solve the issue, contact support through Telegram or email.
HelpCenter.faq_billing_1_a: Open the pricing or wallet flow and choose a supported payment path. Add-ons, promo, gift and referral discounts appear only when enabled for the current release.
HelpCenter.faq_billing_1_q: How do I increase my bandwidth quota?
HelpCenter.faq_billing_2_a: Refund requests are handled through support and finance review. Provider status, provisioning state, usage/support evidence, and the Refund Policy determine the outcome.
HelpCenter.faq_billing_2_q: Are refunds available for inactive cycles?
HelpCenter.faq_billing_3_a: Only payment methods with account, credential, webhook, status, refund, and reconciliation evidence should be enabled. Other provider options stay hidden or disabled.
HelpCenter.faq_getting_started_1_a: Create an account through the website or Telegram, start trial or complete a supported payment, then import the issued QR code, subscription URL, or config into a supported client.
HelpCenter.faq_getting_started_1_q: How do I start my first connection?
HelpCenter.faq_getting_started_2_a: The network accepts connections from Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android clients via supported Xray/Sing-box-compatible profiles.
HelpCenter.faq_getting_started_3_a: Yes, but the limit depends on the selected plan and backend enforcement. The dashboard and support team should show the active device limit.
HelpCenter.faq_getting_started_3_q: Can I connect multiple devices simultaneously?
HelpCenter.faq_intro: Browse setup, routing, billing, and security answers without waiting for support.
HelpCenter.faq_security_1_a: CyberVPN does not make an independent audited no-logs claim. Operational metadata can exist for security, payments, support, provisioning, observability, and abuse handling.
HelpCenter.faq_security_2_a: CyberVPN uses the encryption and transport modes provided by the approved Xray/Remnawave profiles. Public crypto claims stay limited to what the actual profile evidence confirms.
HelpCenter.faq_security_3_a: Paid state must be preserved, provisioning must retry or escalate, and support must review paid-but-no-access/orphan cases before they exceed the 24-hour policy.
HelpCenter.faq_security_3_q: What if my paid VPN access is not ready?
HelpCenter.faq_troubleshooting_1_a: Check whether the selected region is enabled, try the alternate profile if available, and contact support if the dashboard shows provisioning or payment review.
HelpCenter.faq_troubleshooting_1_q: Why is my connection speed degraded?
HelpCenter.faq_troubleshooting_2_a: Use the alternate subscription profile when available. If access was paid but VPN is not ready, support must escalate the case under the paid entitlement escalation policy.
HelpCenter.faq_troubleshooting_2_q: What if the primary proxy fails?
HelpCenter.faq_troubleshooting_3_a: CyberVPN uses VLESS Reality RAW as the default profile and VLESS Reality XHTTP where configured. Results depend on the user network and current node evidence.
HelpCenter.faq_troubleshooting_3_q: How do I handle DPI restrictions?
HelpCenter.hero_subtitle: Find setup, billing, security, and connection guidance.
HelpCenter.meta_description: Troubleshooting, setup guidance, billing support, and public release support escalation channels.

protocols.desc: CyberVPN exposes VLESS Reality RAW as default and VLESS Reality XHTTP as the alternate path. Other transports stay out of default public scope until separately approved.

ram.desc: Public privacy wording is limited to what current evidence can support. Operational metadata may exist for security, billing, support, and provisioning.

routing.desc: Region visibility is evidence-gated: a location is shown only when node health, profile mapping, and support handling are ready.
routing.title: Intelligent Routing

features.sectionSubtitle: Six layers of defense engineered for the modern cyber-citizen

stealth.desc: VLESS Reality is the default path for networks where standard VPN fingerprints are unreliable. Availability depends on real node evidence.
stealth.title: Stealth Mode (Reality)

hero.protocol_badge: PUBLIC RELEASE PROTOCOLS: VLESS REALITY RAW + VLESS REALITY XHTTP | CORE: XRAY-CORE
hero.subtitle: Public B2C VPN access with evidence-gated regions, bounded privacy claims, and monitored support recovery paths.
hero.title: PUBLIC VPN ACCESS. POWERED BY VLESS REALITY

how_it_works.desc: Reality-based profiles are configured to resemble ordinary HTTPS flows while keeping rollout evidence-driven. XHTTP is treated as an alternate path, not as a universal bypass promise.
how_it_works.title: How Reality Works

quick_start.step2: Activate trial or choose a plan

quick_start.terminalStatus: > VPN access ready when provisioning and support recovery pass.

seoCopy.primary: CyberVPN publishes only regions that pass node health, subscription delivery, rollback, support, and abuse-control checks.
seoCopy.secondary: VLESS Reality and XHTTP access is issued through monitored subscription links, with user-facing status and support before beta capacity expands.

Network.description: Public network overview with current reach, utilization, and service health derived from sanitized telemetry.

dpi.description: A careful public surface for restrictive-network resilience. This page only publishes a score when there is enough fresh probe signal to support it.
dpi.disabledState: Public DPI scoring remains gated until enough real probe signal exists across recent measurement windows.
dpi.enabledState: Fresh enough probe coverage is available, so country-level DPI score output can be published.
dpi.error: The DPI resistance contract is currently unavailable.

items.conditionsChange: Restrictive-country conditions can change quickly and confidence must reflect that.
items.directional: Any published score is directional evidence, not a guarantee.
items.noInternals: No anti-blocking internals, probe identities, or sensitive topology.
items.noMagic: No decorative public score without enough real probes.

guardrails.title: What this page will not fake

items.baseline: HTTPS/SNI application baseline where TLS-capable surfaces exist.
items.connection: Connection success rate under restrictive conditions.
items.freshness: Freshness, sample count, and directional confidence before publication.
items.handshake: Median handshake latency for supported protocols.
items.survival: Session survival and reconnect stability after establishment.

methodology.title: What the public score must eventually reflect

reasonCodes.public_dpi_probe_dimensions_insufficient: Real probes are flowing, but the signal dimensions are still too narrow for a publishable public score.
reasonCodes.public_dpi_probe_pipeline_not_enabled: The public DPI pipeline is wired, but probe publication is not active yet.
reasonCodes.public_dpi_probe_source_unavailable: Probe collection is temporarily degraded, so the public DPI surface remains gated.
reasonCodes.public_dpi_probe_targets_unavailable: Probe targets are currently unavailable for a trustworthy country-level snapshot.
reasonCodes.public_network_regions_unavailable: Regional network data is temporarily unavailable for the DPI surface.

dpi.title: DPI Resistance Score

items.a: Yes. Baseline VPN protection is not tiered away. Paid plans differ by seats, routing modes, server pool quality, dedicated IP access, and support.
items.q: Are all paid plans full VPN plans?

items.a: S1 avoids a public hard-cap promise. Traffic handling follows fair-use rules, node policy, abuse controls, and the selected plan instead of an absolute unlimited claim.
Invite bundles are a term-level bonus. Short terms can have none, while 180-day and 365-day plans can include invite codes with defined friend days and expiry windows.
No. It is a premium option. You can buy it as an add-on on paid plans, and Max already includes one dedicated IP.
items.q: Is Dedicated IP a separate plan?

matrix.subtitle: Core VPN security stays on every paid plan. The difference is device count, routing surface, premium access, invite bundles, and support.
matrix.title: What Actually Changes Between Plans

Pricing.subtitle: 4 plans, 4 billing terms. One full VPN baseline on every paid plan.

summary.noInviteBonus: No invite bundle on this term.

basic.description: A full VPN baseline for personal use with simple routing, no hard data cap messaging, and a healthier default than a single-device tariff.

max.description: The premium top tier. More seats, stronger server access, and one included dedicated IP so the upsell is obvious instead of cosmetic.

plus.description: The mainline plan for phones, laptops, and normal multi-device life. Stealth mode lands here because it solves real networks, not niche edge cases.

pro.description: Built for heavier use: stronger routing access, manual and advanced profiles, and a support posture that fits users who actually notice network quality.

Pricing.title: Pricing That Stays Clear

dataCollection.content: CyberVPN processes account, authentication, Telegram/OAuth linking, payment, subscription, wallet, provisioning, support, security, audit, notification, webhook and observability data needed to operate the service. The exact public list must match the deployed backend, Remnawave, provider and support evidence.
dataCollection.title: 02. DATA CATEGORIES

encryption.content: Security relies on hashed passwords, hashed refresh tokens, encrypted selected secrets/tokens where implemented, production admin 2FA, RBAC, audit logs, private PostgreSQL, private Valkey/Redis, private Remnawave API and HTTPS-only public ingress. Stronger crypto or no-logs claims require published evidence.
encryption.title: 04. SECURITY CONTROLS

introduction.content: This page summarizes CyberVPN privacy posture and links conceptually to the full Privacy Policy. Public claims are limited to what the current backend, Remnawave, node, support, payment and observability evidence can support.
introduction.title: 01. PRIVACY SUMMARY

noLogs.content: CyberVPN app/backend is not designed to store browsing content, visited website content, DNS query content or raw VPN traffic content. This is not an independent audited no-logs certification: operational metadata may exist for security, payments, support, provisioning, observability and abuse handling.
noLogs.title: 03. NO-LOGS LIMITS

thirdParties.content: CyberVPN may use payment providers, Telegram, Google/GitHub OAuth, Remnawave, VPN node hosts, email/support tooling and observability providers. Enabled providers need privacy/refund wording, credentials, webhook/status evidence and PII controls.
thirdParties.title: 05. PROVIDER BOUNDARIES

meta.description: Public Release 1.0 privacy notice covering data categories, retention criteria, no-logs limits, providers and privacy contacts.
meta.title: Privacy Policy - CyberVPN

changes.description: CyberVPN may update this policy when providers, product flows, retention rules, support processes or legal requirements change. Material public changes should stay aligned with the Terms of Service, Acceptable Use Policy, Refund Policy and Cookie Policy.
changes.title: Changes to This Policy

contact.addressLine: CyberVPN project owner, individual founder/owner. General support: support@cyber-vpn.net. Abuse reports: abuse@cyber-vpn.net. Refund requests: refund@cyber-vpn.net.
contact.intro: For privacy questions, deletion/export requests or privacy-related complaints, contact us through the approved privacy route:
contact.title: Privacy Contact

dataCollection.intro: CyberVPN processes the data needed to operate the public B2C service, authenticate users, provision VPN access, process payments, prevent abuse and provide support:

items.accountInfo: Account and authentication data: email, username/display name, password hash, 2FA/TOTP state, Telegram ID/username where linked, OAuth provider identifiers and profile email where used.
items.deviceInfo: Device, access and provisioning data: session identifiers, refresh-token metadata, device ID where provided, IP address and user-agent in auth/security logs, Remnawave user reference, subscription URL state and device credential state.
items.paymentInfo: Commercial and payment data: plan, trial, subscription, wallet state, provider name, provider transaction identifiers, payment status, refund/dispute metadata and reconciliation records.
items.usageStats: Operational data: support tickets/staff notes, notification delivery state, audit logs, webhook payload snapshots, security/rate-limit events, backup presence and aggregated service health/traffic counters where enabled.

dataCollection.title: Data We Process

dataRetention.description: Retention periods depend on account status, security needs, provider obligations, abuse handling, support requirements and backup windows:

policies.accountData: Account, authentication and subscription data: retained while the account is active and for the period needed to handle security, abuse, accounting, support, deletion and backup windows.
policies.deletedAccounts: Validated deletion requests: operational account deletion target is 30 days, except records that must be retained for payment, security, legal, fraud, backup or abuse handling. Production PostgreSQL backup retention follows the approved backup policy.
policies.paymentRecords: Payment, refund, dispute and reconciliation records: retained for provider, finance, tax, fraud-prevention and legal obligations; the practical period depends on the enabled provider rules and applicable obligations.
policies.supportLogs: Support tickets, staff notes and notification records: target retention is 90 days after closure unless needed for fraud, abuse, paid-no-access, legal, safety or accounting reasons.

dataRetention.title: Retention Criteria

dataSecurity.description: CyberVPN uses operational security controls for the public B2C service and limits public claims to what the deployed system can support:

measures.accessControl: Admin access is role-gated, requires 2FA in production mode and writes audit events for privileged operations.
measures.encryption: Passwords and refresh tokens are stored as hashes; selected OAuth/TOTP/provider tokens are encrypted or handled through the approved secret process where implemented.
measures.regularAudits: Release readiness uses evidence for secret scanning, dependency checks, backup/restore, rollback, observability, webhook idempotency, support escalation and admin access control. No independent public security audit is claimed unless CyberVPN publishes one explicitly.
measures.secureProtocols: Production topology uses private PostgreSQL, private Valkey/Redis, private Remnawave API and HTTPS-only public ingress where required.

dataSecurity.title: Security Controls

gdprRights.intro: Depending on your location and applicable law, you may have rights to request access, correction, deletion, portability, objection or restriction. Some requests may require manual support/privacy processing.

access.description: Request a copy or summary of personal data associated with your account.
access.title: Access

erasure.description: Request account deletion subject to payment, security, legal, fraud, backup and abuse-retention limits.
erasure.title: Deletion

objection.description: Object to or request restriction of optional processing where applicable. Service-critical processing may be required to provide VPN access.
objection.title: Objection or Restriction

portability.description: Request a portable account-data export where technically available or a manual support export.
portability.title: Portability

rectification.description: Request correction of inaccurate account, Telegram/OAuth or support data.
rectification.title: Correction

gdprRights.title: Privacy Rights

noLogPolicy.details: This is not an independent audited no-logs certification. Security, payment, support, provisioning, node and observability records may contain operational data such as account identifiers, IP address, user-agent, provider IDs, timestamps, aggregated counters or error metadata where needed to operate, secure, support and reconcile the service.

notLogged.browsing: Browsing content and visited website content are not intended to be stored by the CyberVPN app/backend.
notLogged.connections: Connection/session metadata depends on Remnawave, VPN node and observability configuration and is kept within the operational limits described in this policy.
notLogged.dns: DNS query logging is not advertised as an audited no-logs guarantee; actual handling depends on the active node, resolver and client configuration.
notLogged.traffic: Raw VPN traffic content is not intended to be inspected by CyberVPN support/admin tooling.

noLogPolicy.statement: CyberVPN app/backend is not designed to store browsing content, visited website content, DNS query content or raw VPN traffic content.
noLogPolicy.title: VPN Activity and No-Logs Limits

thirdParty.description: CyberVPN may use the following provider categories where enabled and configured with the required operational controls:

services.analytics: Operational analytics/observability such as Sentry, PostHog or metrics/logging tools only where enabled and configured with PII controls.
services.infrastructure: Infrastructure and service providers: managed PostgreSQL, private Valkey/Redis, Remnawave, VPN node hosts, Telegram, Google/GitHub OAuth and email/support tooling.
services.payment: Payment providers: PayRam, NOWPayments, CryptoBot, Telegram Stars for Telegram, Digiseller and YooKassa where approved for the relevant user segment.

thirdParty.title: Processors and Providers

RefundPolicy.intro: This Refund Policy explains how refund requests are handled for CyberVPN accounts, trial-to-paid flows, Telegram Bot, Telegram Mini App, web checkout and VPN access provisioned through CyberVPN.

meta.description: Public Release 1.0 Refund Policy for CyberVPN.
meta.title: Refund Policy

items.fullRefund: A full refund normally cancels or disables the paid subscription period associated with that payment.
items.partialRefund: A partial refund may reduce subscription time, quota, wallet balance or other paid entitlement, depending on the approved correction.
items.pending: A pending refund request does not automatically pause, extend or disable VPN access unless support/ops applies a separate correction.
items.trial: Trial access remains governed by the trial policy and does not create a cash refund entitlement.

changes.content: CyberVPN may update this policy when provider rules, product flows, payment methods, support processes or legal requirements change. Changes should stay aligned with the Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy and Cookie Policy.
changes.title: 8. Changes

evidence.content: CyberVPN may keep payment, refund, support, audit and provider evidence needed to review the request, prevent fraud, reconcile provider records, respond to disputes and satisfy legal or tax obligations. This must align with the Privacy Policy and data-retention controls.
evidence.title: 7. Evidence and Records

items.cryptobot: CryptoBot/Crypto Pay: paid invoices are final provider states for access, but refunds require manual support/finance action, transfer/check evidence or another approved provider workflow. No automatic refund is promised.
items.digiseller: Digiseller: refund handling follows Digiseller purchase/dispute flow, seller balance rules and provider status evidence. Digital goods and delivered credentials require manual review before any refund decision.
items.nowpayments: NOWPayments: completed and partially paid crypto transactions generally leave provider custody; CyberVPN must review merchant-side refund responsibility, wrong-asset/wrong-network/minimum-amount exceptions and any NOWPayments validation requirements.
items.payram: PayRam: crypto refunds are not automatic chargebacks. Refunds must be handled manually through the merchant-controlled dashboard/API or direct wallet transfer, with wallet-address validation and finance approval.
items.telegramStars: Telegram Stars: refund handling is available only for Telegram Bot/Mini App paid flow after CyberVPN stores the Telegram payment charge ID and proves the refundStarPayment path. The bot must support /paysupport for payment issues.
items.yookassa: YooKassa: refunds can be created for successful payments where the payment method supports it. Full or partial refund handling must use provider evidence, idempotency controls, receipt/fiscalization rules where required and finance approval.

request.content: Send refund requests to refund@cyber-vpn.net or through the approved Telegram/support flow. Include your CyberVPN account email or Telegram ID, payment ID or provider invoice ID, payment date, payment method/provider and a short reason for the request. Do not send passwords, 2FA codes, full card numbers, CVV/CVC, seed phrases, raw QR codes, raw subscription URLs or raw VPN configuration files.
request.title: 2. How to Request a Refund

items.abuse: Fraud, chargeback abuse, policy violations, account resale, credential sharing, malware/spam abuse or other Acceptable Use Policy violations may reduce or remove refund eligibility where allowed by applicable law.
items.duplicate: Duplicate charges, confirmed overpayments, wrong amount or provider-confirmed processing errors are reviewable cases.
items.fees: Blockchain network fees, provider fees, conversion spread, exchange fees, bank fees, fiscal/receipt obligations and third-party fees may be non-refundable unless a provider or applicable law requires otherwise.
items.manualReview: Refunds are handled through finance/support review unless a provider-specific automated path is enabled and evidenced.
items.noGuarantee: Submitting a request does not guarantee a refund. CyberVPN must verify the payment status, provider rules, provisioning status and support evidence before a refund is approved or denied.
items.paidNoAccess: Paid-but-no-access or orphan payment cases must be escalated for manual review. No unresolved paid-but-no-access/orphan payment may be older than 24 hours without owner/support escalation.
items.serviceFault: If CyberVPN cannot provision or restore paid VPN access after reasonable support and retry steps, CyberVPN may provide a refund, service extension or another correction approved by finance/ops.

scope.content: This policy applies to public B2C purchases and payment attempts made through PayRam, NOWPayments, CryptoBot, Telegram Stars, Digiseller and YooKassa where those providers are enabled. Refunds are reviewed against payment provider status, CyberVPN subscription state, provisioning state, support evidence, abuse checks and applicable legal requirements.
scope.title: 1. Scope

timing.content: CyberVPN aims to acknowledge refund/payment support requests within the published support targets. Actual refund timing depends on manual review, provider status, blockchain or bank settlement, provider validation and any additional information required from the user.
timing.title: 6. Timing

RefundPolicy.title: Refund Policy

classified.text1: Our [OBFUSCATION] algorithms were engineered by former [REDACTED] architects, allowing our traffic to blend seamlessly with the background noise of the internet.
classified.text2: In the event of a physical server seizure, the [CONTAGION PROTOCOL] automatically zero-fills all disk arrays [REDACTED].
classified.title: TOP SECRET

Security.description: ABSOLUTE PROTECTION. INITIALIZING PROTOCOLS OF BLISSFUL IGNORANCE...

bareMetal.description: We utilize exclusively leased bare-metal servers. No VPS neighbors. Memory operates in RAM-only mode (Tails-like architecture), completely annihilating all data upon power cycle.
bareMetal.title: 01. BARE METAL TIER

client.description: Open-source applications subjected to rigorous independent audits. Embedded Network Lock (Kill Switch) instantly severs traffic upon the slightest loss of core connection.
client.title: 04. CLIENT TIER

crypto.description: AES-256-GCM cipher with Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) via ECDHE (Curve25519) key exchange. Even if math breaks tomorrow, yesterday's traffic remains illegible.
crypto.title: 03. CRYPTOGRAPHY

network.description: Complex Anycast DNS and traffic obfuscation engine. Your packets are masked as standard HTTPS traffic (TLS 1.3), rendering the VPN connection invisible to state firewalls (DPI).
network.title: 02. NETWORK TIER

Security.title: ENCRYPTION CORE

config.description: Use the subscription URL first. It keeps clients synced when routes, credentials, or stealth profiles rotate.
config.downloadNote: The file is generated only in your browser from the active delivery payload.

empty.missing_config: The backend has not returned a config yet. Retry after provisioning finishes or check the subscription state.
empty.missing_profile: Profile data is unavailable, so the user-scoped config cannot be requested.
empty.missing_service: Service access is not fully provisioned yet. Finish plan activation or device delivery first.
empty.not_found: The upstream subscription record was not found for this account.

config.safeDelivery: Full values are hidden on screen. Use copy, QR, or download only on a trusted device.
config.title: One-tap VPN import

Terms.description: CyberVPN Public Release 1.0 service terms for accounts, trials, paid plans, support and VPN access.

acceptance.content: These Terms govern access to CyberVPN through cyber-vpn.net, Telegram Bot, Telegram Mini App and related customer interfaces. cyber-vpn.org is used only where CyberVPN presents VPN node or subscription delivery routes. By creating an account, starting a trial, paying for a plan or using the VPN service, you agree to these Terms and the linked Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy, Refund Policy and Cookie Policy. The legal seller category for this release is the CyberVPN project owner, individual founder/owner. Public support, privacy, abuse and refund contacts are published through CyberVPN support channels.
acceptance.title: 01. Agreement and seller

liability.content: You are responsible for how you use the service, for complying with laws that apply to you, and for keeping your account, devices and configuration links secure. The service is provided as-is and as-available. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, CyberVPN is not responsible for indirect, incidental, special, consequential or lost-profit damages, provider outages, third-party blocking, device issues or unlawful user conduct. Nothing in these Terms limits rights or liabilities that cannot legally be limited.
liability.title: 04. Responsibility and limitations

prohibited.content: CyberVPN is provided for lawful private network access, privacy protection and resilience on restricted or untrusted networks. You must not use the service to harm other systems, abuse third parties, violate applicable law or bypass provider restrictions. The following activity is prohibited:

rules.abuse: Attacks against networks, services, devices or accounts, including DDoS, flooding and unauthorized scanning
rules.illegal: Illegal activity, rights infringement or activity that exposes CyberVPN, its users or providers to legal or network abuse risk
rules.malware: Malware distribution, botnet command and control, exploit hosting or other malicious infrastructure
rules.minorSafety: Child sexual abuse material or any content exploiting minors
rules.restrictedTraffic: Traffic prohibited by a specific node, provider, payment method, sanctions/export restriction or published node policy
rules.spam: Spam, phishing, credential stuffing, fraud, impersonation or bulk unsolicited messaging

prohibited.title: 02. Acceptable use

service.content: CyberVPN Public Release 1.0 provides public B2C access through the approved website, Telegram and backend provisioning flows. Features, regions, nodes, speeds, payment methods, Telegram flows and provisioning paths may be limited, changed, paused or removed for security, provider, support or operational reasons. Trial access and paid access are provisioned through the approved CyberVPN flow and Remnawave-backed VPN access. Paid access starts only after a provider final-success payment state is verified. Automatic renewal is available only where the checkout explicitly says so and the provider flow has been enabled with user consent. Do not rely on any uptime, speed, location, no-logs, privacy or unblock claim unless it is stated in the public policy or product surface and supported by the current service evidence.
service.title: 03. Service, trial and billing

termination.content: CyberVPN may suspend, limit or terminate access for non-payment, abuse, security risk, provider requirement, violation of these Terms or emergency operational need. Where practical, support will use the approved support path before final action, but urgent abuse, legal or infrastructure-risk cases may be escalated immediately. Refund requests are handled through refund@cyber-vpn.net under the Refund Policy and provider-specific rules. Abuse reports go to abuse@cyber-vpn.net. General support is available through support@cyber-vpn.net and Telegram support flows. Do not share passwords, 2FA codes, full card numbers, CVV/CVC, raw QR codes, raw subscription URLs or raw config files in support chats.
termination.title: 05. Suspension, termination and support

Terms.title: Terms of Service
